_metadata.source: Compiled from OCP, NAPRA, USP <795>, WHMIS/GHS regulatory documents
_metadata.description: Offline regulatory reference data used by the rule engine for risk assessment generation. All data stored locally so no real-time API calls needed.

ocp_rules.documentTitle: Non-Sterile Compounding Risk Assessment Companion Guide

A.label: Level A – Low Risk
A.description: For non-hazardous preparations with low complexity. Standard compounding area with general ventilation.

B.label: Level B – Moderate Risk
B.description: For occasional small quantities of hazardous drugs (NIOSH Table 2/3) OR moderate complexity preparations.
requirements: Dedicated compounding area with restricted access
Documented cleaning protocol for hazardous drugs
Disposable hazardous drug gown (closed front, elastic cuffs)
B.notes: NIOSH Table 2/3 drugs permitted only when compounding frequency qualifies as 'occasional small quantity' per OCP definition.

C.label: Level C – High Risk
C.description: For NIOSH Table 1 drugs, high-frequency hazardous drug compounding, or when volume exceeds occasional small quantity thresholds.
requirements: Biological Safety Cabinet (BSC) or Containment Ventilated Enclosure (CVE)
N95 respirator minimum; PAPR recommended for extended sessions
Hazardous drug spill kit (Level C specific)
Annual competency assessments for hazardous drug handling
C.notes: Required for all NIOSH Table 1 drugs and for Table 2/3 drugs compounded at frequency exceeding occasional small quantity.

occasionalSmallQuantityDefinition.description: OCP defines 'occasional small quantity' based on cumulative compounding frequency and volume. Daily compounding of hazardous drugs does NOT qualify regardless of batch size.
occasionalSmallQuantityDefinition.notes: If batch size exceeds 50 units per session or frequency is daily, reassess for Level C. The pharmacist must exercise professional judgment considering total cumulative exposure.

riskLevelRules.condition: No NIOSH-listed ingredients AND complexity is Simple or Moderate
riskLevelRules.rationale: Low-risk preparation with no hazardous drug exposure concerns.

riskLevelRules.condition: NIOSH Table 2 or 3 ingredient AND occasional small quantity AND adequate engineering controls
riskLevelRules.rationale: Hazardous drug present but mitigated by low frequency/volume and appropriate controls.
Antineoplastic drug requires highest level of containment regardless of frequency.

riskLevelRules.condition: NIOSH Table 2 ingredient AND daily compounding frequency
riskLevelRules.rationale: Daily exposure to hazardous drug exceeds occasional small quantity threshold.

riskLevelRules.condition: Multiple NIOSH Table 2/3 ingredients in same preparation
riskLevelRules.rationale: Cumulative hazard from multiple hazardous ingredients may warrant Level C. Professional judgment required.

requiredAssessmentFields: Ingredient hazard assessment (NIOSH, SDS, WHMIS for each ingredient)
Complexity classification (Simple, Moderate, Complex) with justification
Occasional small quantity determination (for NIOSH-listed drugs)
Exposure risk analysis (skin, eye, inhalation, oral)
Risk level assignment (A, B, or C) with detailed rationale
Next review date (12 months from approval)

triggerEvents: New hazard information (NIOSH update, SDS revision)
Change in compounding frequency or volume
Regulatory update (OCP, NAPRA, or USP change)
Incident or near-miss related to preparation

napra_complexity.source: NAPRA Model Standards for Pharmacy Compounding of Non-Sterile Preparations

SIMPLE.label: Simple
SIMPLE.description: Reconstitution or mixing of commercially available products according to manufacturer directions, or preparations involving straightforward mixing of a few ingredients.
criteria: Follows a well-established formulation (published, tested)
Uses commercially available bases/vehicles
Straightforward mixing or incorporation
No specialized equipment beyond standard compounding tools
No critical steps where deviation affects product quality
Commonly: creams, ointments, simple solutions, basic suspensions
SIMPLE.beyondUseDate: Up to 180 days based on stability data or USP <795> defaults

MODERATE.label: Moderate
MODERATE.description: Preparations requiring specific technique, equipment, or environmental controls. May involve calculations, multi-step processes, or dosage form modifications.
criteria: Requires specific compounding technique (e.g., levigation, fusion)
May require temperature control during preparation
Involves precise measurements and calculations
May require specialized equipment (e.g., suppository molds, capsule machine)
Multi-step process with critical control points
Trained personnel required
Commonly: suppositories, capsules, suspensions requiring particle size reduction
MODERATE.beyondUseDate: Based on stability data; typically 90-180 days

COMPLEX.label: Complex
COMPLEX.description: Preparations requiring advanced technique, extensive calculation, specialized equipment, or posing specific quality challenges.
criteria: Advanced compounding technique required
Extensive calculations (e.g., alligation, weight/volume conversions)
Specialized equipment mandatory (e.g., tablet press, ointment mill)
Multiple critical steps where deviations significantly affect quality
May require in-process testing or quality checks
Stability challenges requiring specific studies
Commonly: tablets, controlled-release formulations, transdermal systems
COMPLEX.beyondUseDate: Must be supported by specific stability data

H314.text: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage

H317.text: May cause an allergic skin reaction

H334.text: May cause allergy or asthma symptoms or breathing difficulties if inhaled

H360.text: May damage fertility or the unborn child

H361.text: Suspected of damaging fertility or the unborn child

H362.text: May cause harm to breast-fed children

ghsCategoryDescriptions.1A: Known human reproductive toxicant (based on human evidence)
ghsCategoryDescriptions.1B: Presumed human reproductive toxicant (based on animal evidence)
ghsCategoryDescriptions.2: Suspected human reproductive toxicant (based on limited evidence)

beyondUseDateDefaults.description: Default BUD assignments when no stability data is available

aqueous.notes: Aqueous or water-containing formulations without stability data

with_stability_data.notes: Extended BUD permitted with supporting stability data from testing lab

qualityControlRequirements: Weight verification for individual units (capsules, suppositories)
Documentation of all quality checks in compounding record
